scope: RL2: W==D with reg-accum + LDS reclaim (group_segment 10240)
finding: The LDS reclaim (group 12288->10240, 5->6 wg/CU) is RESOURCE-CORRECT but moves W==D ~0 at both ctx. ctx4096's neutrality was therefore NOT caused by the over-sized group_segment -- it is deeper: the tile is ~10% of decode wall (N3F) and is not 5->6-wg/CU occupancy-bound (consistent with Phase M's 4->5 wg/CU = ~0). Net feature state: ctx512 +5.4%, ctx4096 neutral, resource-correct (VGPR 56, LDS 10240, DS 31->9). PC/source lds_accum DS 31->9 confirms the attribution row moved; full N4 re-run not required (W==D is the decisive metric and shows no further movement).
next: reg-accum is correct + resource-correct + net-positive but NOT owned parity (68.1%/59.8%). Remaining ctx4096 gap is wall-share/algorithmic, not a tile resource knob. Feature stays default-off opt-in.
